svg.text: Self-Project Team vs Strategic ASIC Partner
3 decisive reasons each — and why the answer is a phased hybrid
Self-Project Team — MUST
Strategic ASIC Partner — MUST
1. Model-silicon co-design requires internal IP
Attention patterns, KV cache pressure, MoE routing, speculative
decoding are OpenAI crown jewels. Embedding them into silicon
requires tight iteration between model researchers and chip architects.
Broadcom never sees these internals by definition.
Evidence: Brockman — "We embed what frontier research teaches directly into silicon."
1. Partner IP cannot be replicated in time
Broadcom's 3.5D XDSiP, SerDes, HBM PHY took 5+ years to build.
A 40-person team cannot replicate before 2026 window closes.
Google, after a decade of TPU work, still licenses Broadcom SerDes
for LLM training — even with full RTL ownership of compute die.
Evidence: Broadcom's 3.5D XDSiP validated in silicon; one XPU ships H2 2026.
2. IP ownership breaks partner rent-extraction
Google TPU is the warning: deep dependency let Broadcom raise
TPU design prices ~30% and capture ~70% margins. OpenAI
recreating Nvidia dependency under Broadcom's logo defeats the
vertical integration thesis entirely. RTL must be OpenAI-owned.
Evidence: Google planned to cut Broadcom from TPU post-2027 after 30% price hike.
2. Full-stack design needs 1000s, not 40 engineers
PHY, SerDes, HBM interface, DFT, physical implementation,
EDA flows, packaging — each is a separate discipline requiring
specialist teams. Google TPU team = hundreds of engineers plus
Broadcom's thousands, and still doesn't go it alone.
Evidence: Omdia — "Only a handful of enterprises can design AI hardware with internal SW support."
3. Roadmap autonomy: pre-build next-gen primitives
Chip cycles are 18-24 months. OpenAI's next model architecture
change must be pre-embedded in Jalapeño's successor now. Only
an internal team with model roadmap access can do this. If
Broadcom sets the cadence, OpenAI is always one gen behind.
Evidence: Titan 2 (A16) in planning concurrently with Titan 1 (N3) production.
3. Packaging and foundry remain partner-dependent
TSMC fabricates ~92% of AI chips at 7nm and below — non-
substitutable. 3.5D XDSiP is Broadcom's proprietary stack. Even
with full RTL ownership, OpenAI still needs Broadcom-class
packaging. True independence at current headcount is an illusion.
Evidence: TSMC manufactures Google TPU, Trainium, Maia, MTIA, Jalapeño — all of them.
Synthesis:
In-house team owns compute die RTL and compiler co-design. Broadcom executes PHY, SerDes, packaging. The boundary matches exactly where Google
draws the line after a decade of TPU development. Inflection toward greater internalization comes when OpenAI's team scales to ~200-300 engineers.
Fig 4 · Self-Project Team vs Strategic ASIC Partner | momosan2692.github.io
